# DREAM Cycle: Steve's Product Ideas
**Date:** 2026-04-13
**Perspective:** Design Quality & User Delight
**Board Member:** Steve

---

## Idea 1: **Changelog Theatre**

**What:** A WordPress plugin that transforms boring changelogs into beautiful, animated release pages using Remotion. Each version update becomes a 30-second video with TTS narration, auto-generated from your plugin's readme.txt or changelog file.

**User:** WordPress plugin developers who want to make updates feel like events, not chores.

**Distribution:** WordPress.org plugin directory, Product Hunt, indie hacker communities.

**Delight Factor:** Instead of "Fixed bug in settings page," users see a polished video: "We heard you. Settings now remember your choices." with smooth animations and a calm voice. Updates become moments of trust-building.

**Technical Fit:** Remotion expertise + TTS + WordPress plugin development. One session buildable.

**Dogfood:** We use it for our own plugins (Dash, Pinned).

---

## Idea 2: **Commit Mood**

**What:** A VS Code extension + web dashboard that analyzes your commit messages and code changes to visualize the "emotional arc" of your project. Uses Claude to detect sentiment, urgency, and frustration levels. Shows when you were in flow vs. fighting bugs.

**User:** Solo developers and small teams who want to understand their work patterns.

**Distribution:** VS Code marketplace, indie dev Twitter, Show HN.

**Delight Factor:** A beautiful timeline that shows your project's story. "March was your most creative month. You were in flow 68% of the time." Makes invisible work visible.

**Technical Fit:** Claude AI integration + simple web dashboard. One session.

**Dogfood:** We run it on our own repos to understand agency patterns.

---

## Idea 3: **Poster Child**

**What:** A one-click tool that turns any GitHub README into a beautiful social preview image + Open Graph card. Paste a repo URL, get a stunning poster that captures the essence of the project.

**User:** Open source maintainers who want their projects to look professional on social media.

**Distribution:** Direct link sharing, GitHub Actions marketplace, Product Hunt.

**Delight Factor:** Your scrappy side project suddenly looks like it has a design team. The poster captures key stats, language breakdown, and generates a tagline using Claude.

**Technical Fit:** Cloudflare Workers + Claude + image generation. One session.

**Dogfood:** We use it for all shipyard-ai repos.

---

*"Design is not just what it looks like. Design is how it works. But also... what it looks like matters."*
— Steve
